sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Casa Pia, sitting 16th in the league with 14 points, have struggled recently, going winless in their last 3 matches with 2 losses and a draw. AVS - Futebol SAD, languishing at the bottom in 18th with just 4 points, are in dire form, losing their last 3 matches without scoring a goal. Both teams are in desperate need of points, but Casa Pia’s slightly better resilience gives them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Casa Pia have the upper hand, remaining unbeaten with a 2-0 win and a 1-1 draw against AVS. Their ability to score against AVS, combined with AVS’s inability to keep a clean sheet in these matchups, suggests Casa Pia are likely to find the net again.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Casa Pia have scored 17 goals this season (15th in the league) but have conceded 35 (16th), showing defensive frailty. AVS have been even worse, with only 11 goals scored and a league-worst 44 conceded, highlighting their vulnerability at both ends. Casa Pia’s marginally better attacking output and home advantage could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Casa Pia, Jérémy Livolant (3 goals, 4 assists, 7.11 avg rating) is a major threat in attack, while AVS’s Óscar Perea (2 goals, 6.63 avg rating) could be their best hope for a goal. Casa Pia’s superior individual quality might tilt the game in their favor.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Casa Pia average 4.06 corners per game and commit many fouls (281 total, 2nd highest), which could lead to a physical battle. AVS’s poor defensive stats (61 big chances created by opponents, worst in the league) suggest they will struggle to contain Casa Pia’s attacks, especially in the final third.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Casa Pia’s better recent head-to-head record, home advantage, and AVS’s ongoing struggles (no wins in 4 matches and no goals in 3), a home win seems the most likely outcome. Sofascore’s prediction of a 42% chance for a Casa Pia victory aligns with this analysis. Expect a tight game with Casa Pia narrowly edging it.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Casa Pia take an early lead with a goal from David Sousa in the 5th minute. Despite having less ball possession compared to AVS - Futebol SAD, Casa Pia made the most of their opportunities, creating a couple of significant chances and converting one. AVS, on the other hand, dominated possession and made numerous entries into the final third and penalty area but failed to capitalize on their efforts. The away side also picked up three yellow cards, indicating a more aggressive or frustrated approach as they chased an equalizer.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Casa Pia started the game with strong attacking intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes and maintaining pressure which led to their early goal. Their momentum was evident in several attacking spells during the first half, particularly in the initial stages. However, as the half progressed, AVS - Futebol SAD took control, showing sustained periods of dominance with consistent attacking waves. Towards the latter part of the half, there were moments where Casa Pia regained some momentum, but AVS largely dictated the tempo with frequent forward surges.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Casa Pia, David Sousa stood out not only for his goal but also for his defensive contributions with multiple clearances and interceptions. Abdu Conté also impressed with a high rating and key contributions in creating chances. On the AVS side, players like Tomané and Óscar Perea showed intent in attack with shots on target and key passes, though they couldn’t find the back of the net. The away team's discipline issues, reflected in the yellow cards, might have hampered their rhythm.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Casa Pia adopted a more direct and efficient approach, focusing on making the most of fewer possession opportunities while maintaining a solid defensive structure. Their ability to win over half of their aerial duels suggests a physical edge that helped them in key moments. AVS - Futebol SAD played with greater control of the ball, focusing on building play through the final third, but their lack of precision in front of goal was evident. Their higher number of penalty area entries indicates potential for a breakthrough if they can sharpen their finishing.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the patterns of play, AVS - Futebol SAD are likely to continue their high-possession game and push for an equalizer with renewed intensity. Casa Pia will probably look to protect their lead, potentially sitting deeper and relying on counter-attacks to exploit any spaces left by AVS. The away team's disciplinary record could play a factor if tempers flare further, possibly creating opportunities for Casa Pia. However, AVS's persistence in attack might eventually pay off if they can convert their entries into clear scoring chances.
